submission.questionnaireText:
Gabe Kelley (Gabriel Kelley)
Adult 12/14/1994 (30)  Schedule appointment  Edit

1. Initial Health Survey (complete at your earliest convenience)
09/13/2025 at 8:17 PM
Note



1. Do you gain weight easily?
(3) This is a fairly bothersome issue for me
2. [IF SO] Where do you typically gain weight on your body?
Around the belly
3. Do you feel "puffy," i.e your fat tends to be soft and feel like water retention?
(4) This is a very bothersome issue for me
4. Do you struggle with unintended weight loss?
(1) I do not experience this
5. [WOMEN ONLY] Do you accumulate cellulite?
Not Applicable
6. Do you struggle with low appetite?
(1) I do not experience this
7. Do you tend to wake up when sleeping?
(2) This is a somewhat bothersome issue for me
8. If you tend to wake up when sleeping, is it: within the first few hours of falling asleep OR later on in the night OR both?
Both
9. If you tend to wake up when sleeping, is it to: urinate OR eat something OR drink something OR seemingly no reason?
Urinate
10. If you tend to wake up when sleeping, do you find that you are hot or sweaty upon waking?
Yes
11. Do you NOT dream?
(5) This is an extremely bothersome issue for me
12. Do you experience nightmares or sleep paralysis?
(1) I do not experience this
13. Do you have trouble falling asleep?
(2) This is a somewhat bothersome issue for me
14. Do you feel like you DON'T get a restful sleep?
(5) This is an extremely bothersome issue for me
15. Do you frequently feel cold, especially at the extremities (fingertips, toes, and nose)?
(2) This is a somewhat bothersome issue for me
16. [WOMEN ONLY] Do you frequently have painful menstrual cramps?
Not Applicable
17. [WOMEN ONLY] Do you frequently have irregular menstruation or suffer from premenstrual syndrome?
Not Applicable
18. Do you suffer from chronic congestion without mucus?
(2) This is a somewhat bothersome issue for me
19. Do you frequently experience mucus buildup in the throat or nose?
(5) This is an extremely bothersome issue for me
20. [IF SO] Is it exacerbated after eating?
Yes
21. Do you suffer from dental problems such as bad breath, cavities, and gum inflammation?
(2) This is a somewhat bothersome issue for me
22. Do you often find that you are irritable or easily agitated?
(3) This is a fairly bothersome issue for me
23. Do you suffer from "brain fog," low energy or feel like you do not have mental clarity?
(5) This is an extremely bothersome issue for me
24. Do you tend to have an "afternoon crash" after eating lunch?
(2) This is a somewhat bothersome issue for me
25. Do you often feel "hyper aware," jittery, or restless?
(1) I do not experience this
26. Do you notice the sensation of your heart pounding or difficulty breathing?
(1) I do not experience this
27. Have you had a loss of pleasure in activities you normally enjoy?
(4) This is a very bothersome issue for me
28. Have you noticed a lack of sexual interest or desire?
(1) I do not experience this
29. [MEN ONLY] Have you lost morning erections?
(4) This is a very bothersome issue for me
30. Do you have skin issues such as dry skin, eczema, or acne?
(3) This is a fairly bothersome issue for me
31. Can you trace a worsening of your symptoms back to a traumatic event?
No
32. Do you have specific food cravings? If so, please list the foods you crave.
Yes
Sugar/Carbs
33. Do you have a history of mental health issues? If so, please list them.
No
34. Do you experience constipation?
(4) This is a very bothersome issue for me
35. Do you experience nausea?
(2) This is a somewhat bothersome issue for me
36. Do you experience bloating (subjective feeling)?
(3) This is a fairly bothersome issue for me
37. Do you experience distention (visible expansion of the abdomen)?
(3) This is a fairly bothersome issue for me
38. Do you experience diarrhea?
(2) This is a somewhat bothersome issue for me
39. Do you experience belching?
(5) This is an extremely bothersome issue for me
40. Do you experience flatulence?
(5) This is an extremely bothersome issue for me
41. Do you experience pain in your upper abdomen?
(2) This is a somewhat bothersome issue for me
42. Do you experience pain in your right upper quadrant?
(1) I do not experience this
43. Do you experience pain in your lower abdomen?
(3) This is a fairly bothersome issue for me
44. Are you aware of any specific foods that trigger your symptoms? If so, please list them.
No
45. Do you experience dry stools?
(1) I do not experience this
46. Do you experience acid reflux?
(5) This is an extremely bothersome issue for me
47. Do your symptoms worsen IMMEDIATELY after eating?
(4) This is a very bothersome issue for me
48. Do your symptoms worsen 1-3 hours after eating?
(3) This is a fairly bothersome issue for me
49. Do symptoms alleviate after a bowel movement?
Sometimes
50. Have you tried anything in the past to alleviate your symptoms? These can be dietary changes, supplements, prescriptions or anything else. If so, please list them, and how helpful (or unhelpful) they were for you. Please be as specific as possible, including specific foods and brands and dosages of supplements, as well as what symptoms they affected.
Yes
keto, low carb, gluten free, zinc, magnesium, electrolytes, betaine pepsin, seleium, nac, vit a, b, c, d, e, and k. Creatine, b1, b12, b6. Sunlight in the morning. BPC 157, KPV. Pecid. Sodium Butyrate, CandiBactin-BR & AR, MSM, Marshmallow Root, Aloe Vera Extract & Slippery Elm Bark
51. Are you currently taking any supplements? If so, please list them and be as specific as possible, including the brand and dosage of any supplement. Please mention how helpful (or unhelpful) you perceive them to be.
Yes
Pure Encapsulation,
Zinc carnosine 75mg,
B complex , Thiamax b1 600mg, b6 300mg, MSM, Mag Glycinate 200mg, BPC 157 1000mcg, KPV 1000mcg, Creatine 10g, deglycyrrhizinated licorice 300mg, Marshmallow Root 50mg, Aloe Vera Extract 100mg & Slippery Elm Bark 200mg, MSM 3400mg 3x per day.
52. Are you currently taking any medications? If so, please list them and be as specific as possible, including the brand and dosage of any medication.
Yes
on and off naltrexone 1.5mg up to 4.5 mg
53. Are you currently diagnosed with any medical conditions? If so, please list them.
No
54. Please describe your diet. What foods do you typically eat, and in what amounts? Are these foods prepared by you, or are they pre-packaged, or from a restaurant? At what times do you typically eat?
Restaurant food 1 - 3 times per week (varies in type), typical breakfast is oatmeal with high fiber jam, bananas, sour dough toast with jam or peanut butter, lunch is usually eggs, great yogurt, or leftover dinner, dinner is usually chicken with rice or potatoes, tacos (ground beef), burgers with sour dough bread and ary fried potatoes.

55. Please describe your lifestyle. Do you get outside often? Do you exercise? How stressful is your day-to-day life due to work or other factors?
Pre-baby = exercise 5x week 1 or 2 hours a day,

Now Post baby = 1.5 hours 3x a week, sometimes 1 hour 2 x a week

56. What are your health primary goals you are looking achieve?
Fix gut problems, fix brain fog, and chronic fatigue

57. Have you ever had food poisoning before? If so, when?
Yes, 2021 got salmonella, lost 30lbs

58. Please list your height, weight and waist measurements.
6' 2" 215-225lbs, 34 inch waist

59. Please list any other information you think would help us help you.
I am 90% positive I have LPR, I don't wake up with a coated tongue but it becomes coated by end of day. I was tested recently for candida and fungus but both cultures came back negative.
submission.takehomeText:
What are your four resting heart beats per minute? Please separate values with commas. [example: 78, 81, 75, 80]
64, 64, 69, 67

How many times did you urinate each day for four days? Please separate values with commas. [example: 6, 6, 8, 7]
7, 8, 8, 6

On average, how foamy was your urine?
(1) Not foamy at all
How many times did you defecate per day for four days? Please separate values with commas. [example: 3, 1, 0, 2]
1, 2,3,2

What Types on the Bristol Stool Scale did each of your bowel movements fall under? Please separate values with commas. [example: Type 2, Type 2, Type 4, Type 5, Type 7, Type 1, Type 6]
Please describe any other abnormalities of the stool observed, such as altered color or undigested food in the stool.
type 4, 5, 6

What were both of your temperature measurements each morning? Please separate values with commas. [example:
98.2, 98.4]

96.8, 97.1, 96.9, 97.1

[WOMEN ONLY] What date did your last period begin? [MEN: SKIP QUESTION]
No answer given.

[WOMEN ONLY] What date did you take your first temperature measurement? [MEN: SKIP QUESTION]
submission.advisorNotesText:
Appointment #2
With: Shannon Sirois 
Billing code: 
consulting
Edit
1:00 PM – 2:00 PM
Progress Note 
Gabe is a 30 y.o. dad that reached out to us for help primarily with his ongoing gut issues, but also because he has unbelievable brain fog that is compromising his function. This call was PAINFUL. This poor guy is struggling badly. I couldn’t get a straight answer out of him about anything. He was all over the place, distracted, couldn’t form complete sentences, etc. I apologize in advance if this is choppy, but I did the best that I could to piece this all together. I think he’s going to be one of those cases where we get his gut cleared up a bit and I will be able to further assess how to help him.

It seems like this all began when he finished up college and started his career. I think he works in finance, it was another thing that was really hard to gather information on because he never really gave me a straight answer but that his job is “stressful” at times. At this point of his life (starting his career) he noticed that gut health was an issue, and it’s also when the brain fog started to happen. This was an ongoing thing, and he did say he’s always been a competitive athlete so he was working out hard and trying to self-treat with keto, gut protocols, etc. but never really hit his stride. Keto helped for a bit, but then he had a huge energy crash so he stopped doing it.

Fast-forward to now and he recently had a baby so he’s not sleeping well, he isn’t working out as much because he has no energy for that and then he’s had two bouts of strep through over the last year. One time in February when they gave him penicillin vk and it cleared up, then again this summer where they gave him ciprofloxacin. I am a bit concerned about this, as we know that this can directly damage the mitochondria and given his personality something is really not right with this guy.

I get the impression that I am missing a lot from his story. Like I said, this call was painful. He would get really agitated when I asked him questions and I think it was because he couldn’t come up with the answer, but I’m not sure if he’s just one of those guys that doesn’t like being asked questions so I tried to tread lightly but I did need SOME info. For instance at one point he told me he had a history of steroid use and went on and on about it, and then I asked him what the form was and the dosage and he told me it was testosterone and he wasn’t sure the dosage. Very confusing! I also noticed he indicated he’s on and off naltrexone but I didn’t dare ask that question because at that point in the call he was visibly agitated. I will message him about that.

He also told me he had started taking ciproheptadine and that has greatly enhanced his sleep, but then when I asked him what about his sleep was affected prior to taking it I could not get an answer. From his intake form it does look like he was being woken up with a need to urinate, and maybe waking up a lot during the night but I did not confirm this verbally. I do know he mentioned his daughter will wake him up at times. I asked if he felt rested once he would wake up, but again I didn’t really get a straight answer. It seems like he at least has a schedule of sleep/wake times.

Another thing to note is that he has stopped caffeine as of recently. I feel like this was probably a bad idea for him given his cognitive function. I would assume caffeine would help him a bit, but he seemed to want to stick it out because he thinks it may be contributing to reflux.

So as of now these are the things I’ve gathered:

Digestive disturbances/bloating

Reflux that may be causing LP

Poor sleep

Significant memory/concentration issues

Severe brain fog

Hx of ciprofloxacin

Recurrent strep infections in throat

Supplements:

Pure Encapsulation,
Zinc carnosine 75mg,
B complex , Thiamax b1 600mg, b6 300mg, MSM, Mag Glycinate 200mg, BPC 157 1000mcg, KPV 1000mcg, Creatine 10g, deglycyrrhizinated licorice 300mg, Marshmallow Root 50mg, Aloe Vera Extract 100mg & Slippery Elm Bark 200mg, MSM 3400mg 3x per day

Also add: butyrate in the morning that he’s taking, and ciproheptadine

Plan:

Getting thyroid, iron panel and cholesterol tested to start with. I think he is severe hypo due to his hx as well as his gut function. Once this is back we will get his report going!
Already sent him links: Masszymes, Acerola cherry powder and Triphala to regulate stool, gut and nutrients profiles while waiting for testing.
Testing came back: High TSH, high Ferritin, high LDL. Testing confirmed what I suspected, high inflammation and likely gut dysbiosis. Antibiotic protocol I’m thinking?
He’s already doing butyrate, zinc carnosine, magnesium, etc. I’m kind of at a loss at this point as to what other gut related things we can do. I added the triphala and acerola cherry as he has really good luck with high dose vit C at one point.
Sleep has seemed to be better with cyproheptadine, so I’m not inclined to mess with that at this point.


---
submission.daltonsFinalNotes:
in the questionnaire section - write “Previous history of ciprofloxacin use” for the implication write “Ciprofloxacin is a fluoroquinolone antibiotic that kills bacteria by blocking their DNA gyrase and topoisomerase IV, enzymes essential for DNA replication. Because mitochondria have their own circular DNA and a topoisomerase II enzyme, ciprofloxacin can also bind there, leading to mtDNA depletion and failure to produce the 13 key ETC subunits encoded in mtDNA. Without those proteins, complexes I, III, IV, and V become unstable, causing electron leakage, ROS overproduction, and collapse of efficient ATP generation.”

minocycline

discontinue supplements

Urolithin A- 500 mg - include this as rationale “Urolithin A is a gut-derived metabolite (from pomegranate, walnuts, ellagitannins) that has been shown in humans to activate mitophagy, the selective clearance of damaged mitochondria. By removing dysfunctional mitochondria, it helps restore a healthier mitochondrial pool and improves ATP production efficiency. In the context of ciprofloxacin-induced ETC damage, Urolithin A could aid recovery by clearing defective, ROS-leaking mitochondria and promoting regeneration of new, functional ones.”

Red light

CoQ10

Creatine

****Just got off a quick call with him and he’s ordered the mino already (still include in the report), so good to go there! Everything Dalton said is perfect. Thank you!

Only other thing to add would be a bifidobacteria probiotic to add AFTER mino please!
